title: dasiphora fruticosa
intro: Dasiphora fruticosa, also known as shrubby cinquefoil, is a deciduous shrub native to mountainous regions of East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Dasiphora fruticosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve cardiovascular health. Dasiphora fruticosa is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, with the dried flowers, leaves, and stems used in herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
